things.name: Royal Palace & Silver Pagoda
things.description: The royal residence with the Silver Pagoda housing a life-sized gold Buddha encrusted with diamonds

things.name: Tuol Sleng Genocide Museum (S-21)
things.description: Former Khmer Rouge prison where 17,000 were tortured - essential and harrowing history

things.name: Choeung Ek Killing Fields
things.description: Memorial to the 17,000 victims executed here during the Khmer Rouge genocide

things.name: National Museum of Cambodia
things.description: World's finest collection of Khmer art with sculptures from Angkor Wat

things.name: Riverside Promenade (Sisowath Quay)
things.description: Sunset drinks on the promenade where the Mekong and Tonle Sap rivers merge

things.name: Central Market (Phsar Thmei)
things.description: 1930s Art Deco market with a distinctive yellow dome selling gold, gems and souvenirs

things.name: Russian Market (Phsar Toul Tom Poung)
things.description: Best local market for handicrafts, silk, Buddha statues and cheap street food

things.name: Wat Phnom
things.description: Hill temple that gave the city its name, with a reclining Buddha and resident monkeys

things.name: Cambodian Living Arts Performance
things.description: Traditional apsara dance and classical Khmer music performed by genocide survivors

things.name: Mekong River Sunset Cruise
things.description: Sunset boat cruise on the great river with Angkor beer and views of the Royal Palace
